steps.title: Place the conjunctive adverb
steps.content: The sentence joins two independent clauses with the conjunctive adverb "on the contrary." Standard punctuation places a semicolon before that connector and a comma after it.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: Choice A has no punctuation before the connector. Choice B puts the semicolon after the connector. Choice C uses only commas and creates a comma splice. The remaining choice uses the correct semicolon-comma pattern. The answer is D.
